executive_summary.question: When does the right of private defence of the body commence, and how long does it continue?
executive_summary.generated_answer:
The right of private defence of the body commences as soon as a reasonable apprehension of danger to the body arises from an attempt or threat to commit the offence, though the offence may not have been committed [Chunk-ID: d3d93d06-b511-4735-9b84-6085daecad6e]. 
It continues as long as such apprehension of danger to the body continues [Chunk-ID: d3d93d06-b511-4735-9b84-6085daecad6e]. 
This means that the right of private defence of the body starts when there is a reasonable fear of harm to the body and lasts until that fear is no longer present [Chunk-ID: d3d93d06-b511-4735-9b84-6085daecad6e].
